_template.name: OpenGRIS: Market Risk (Waterfall)
_template.description: Financial risk calculation with waterfall scaling — burst on-premise first, overflow to ECS cloud. Priority 1 native workers handle primary load; priority 2 ECS handles burst.
_template.author: CalmStudio Contributors

nodes.name: Risk Scheduler
nodes.description: OpenGRIS Scaler scheduler dispatching market risk calculation tasks across on-premise and cloud worker pools

nodes.name: Risk Worker 1
nodes.description: On-premise compute worker for latency-sensitive market risk calculations

nodes.name: Risk Worker 2
nodes.description: On-premise compute worker for latency-sensitive market risk calculations

nodes.name: Risk Worker 3
nodes.description: On-premise compute worker for latency-sensitive market risk calculations

nodes.name: Risk Worker 4
nodes.description: On-premise compute worker for latency-sensitive market risk calculations

nodes.name: On-Premise Worker Manager
nodes.description: Native worker manager — primary tier in the waterfall, handles on-premise compute capacity (priority 1)

nodes.name: ECS Burst Worker Manager
nodes.description: AWS ECS worker manager — secondary tier in the waterfall, handles burst overflow when on-premise capacity is exhausted (priority 2)

nodes.name: Risk Object Store
nodes.description: Object storage for market risk task inputs (portfolios, market data) and outputs (risk vectors, P&L)

nodes.name: Risk Engine Client
nodes.description: Risk engine client submitting portfolio batches to Scaler for distributed market risk calculation

relationships.description: Risk engine client submits market risk calculation tasks to the scheduler (ZeroMQ TCP)
Scheduler reads portfolio inputs and writes risk results via object store
Worker receives risk tasks and returns results to the scheduler (ZeroMQ TCP)
Worker receives risk tasks and returns results to the scheduler (ZeroMQ TCP)
Worker receives risk tasks and returns results to the scheduler (ZeroMQ TCP)
Worker receives risk tasks and returns results to the scheduler (ZeroMQ TCP)
Native worker manager registers with scheduler; spawns on-premise workers as first waterfall tier
ECS worker manager registers with scheduler; launches ECS tasks as second waterfall tier when on-premise is saturated
